scope: Official NVIDIA open-source repositories and official NVIDIA AV/efficiency pages most relevant to frontier autonomous driving, world modeling, deployment, and runtime optimization.
source_policy: Primary sources only: official NVIDIA repositories, official developer pages, official model pages, and official NVIDIA news or documentation pages.
design_laws: Use NVIDIA frontier models as unrestricted teachers, simulators, evaluators, or deployment substrate unless they are proven small and stable enough for the runtime student.
Do not confuse a world model, reasoning model, or simulator with the deployable perception trunk; each belongs at a different level of the stack.
Treat TensorRT and model optimization as a second-stage systems play after the metric frontier is technically sound.

entries.title: NVIDIA DRIVE Alpamayo-R1
entries.core_trick: Use a large open reasoning VLA to inject explicit chain-of-thought-style decision structure and scenario understanding into autonomous driving research and teacher workflows.
entries.intuition: Alpamayo is not the 3D detection trunk. Its value is scenario reasoning, edge-case analysis, and long-horizon teacher behavior that a small deployable perception model will never do directly.
ablation_takeaways: Use it to mine supervision, critiques, or scenario annotations rather than replacing metric-grounded perception baselines.
Reasoning gains do not magically repair weak box geometry.
ablation_nuggets: Use Alpamayo as a planner/critic teacher, not as the runtime detector.
Distill only the parts of behavior that can be operationalized into student supervision or evaluation.
apply_when: you need richer AV reasoning, failure analysis, or long-horizon behavior critique
the teacher side is unconstrained and can afford large multimodal reasoning
avoid_when: you are solving raw 3D localization or calibration errors in the perception stack
you need a tight deployable runtime path right now
entries.integration_pattern: Use as a research control-plane or teacher-side evaluator that critiques scenarios, proposes curricula, and labels planning-sensitive failures.
failure_modes: treating language-side reasoning as a substitute for perception metrics
overfitting the research agenda to narrative explanations instead of measurable geometry
tsqbev_actions: use Alpamayo to critique failure cases and generate richer planner-side research prompts
do not replace official detection gates with reasoning-model judgments

entries.title: Cosmos-Reason2
entries.core_trick: Provide physical common-sense reasoning models that generate long chain-of-thought embodied decisions grounded in world-state understanding.
entries.intuition: Reasoning models are valuable when we need stronger teacher critique, scenario interpretation, and post hoc explanation around physical AI behavior, not when we need a fast detector on the edge.
ablation_takeaways: Best used as teacher/evaluator/control-plane reasoning rather than a direct runtime perception module.
ablation_nuggets: Use to analyze failure cases and propose curriculum or synthetic scene generation targets.
Keep it off the critical student runtime path.
apply_when: the research loop needs deeper physical reasoning for scenario analysis or dataset curation
avoid_when: you are trying to fix immediate detection-quality regressions
entries.integration_pattern: Use in the control plane or offline analysis stack, paired with metric-grounded perception outputs.
failure_modes: using reasoning as a substitute for official evaluation
tsqbev_actions: query Cosmos-Reason2-style ideas when the planner needs physical common-sense hypotheses or scenario critique

entries.title: Cosmos-Predict2.5
entries.core_trick: Model future world evolution in video form so synthetic rollouts and physically plausible future predictions become available for training, curation, and evaluation.
entries.intuition: World prediction helps when we need synthetic variation, temporal stress tests, or future-state supervision. It is a teacher and simulator, not the detector itself.
ablation_takeaways: The value is in simulation and future-state supervision, not direct replacement of perception modules.
ablation_nuggets: Use to create failure-focused synthetic continuations or stress-test scenarios.
Do not put it in the runtime student path.
apply_when: you need richer synthetic or future-conditioned supervision than the dataset currently provides
avoid_when: the active issue is a broken evaluation/export path rather than data coverage
entries.integration_pattern: Use offline for scenario generation, hard-negative expansion, or future-conditioned curriculum design.
failure_modes: simulation drift that does not map back to deployment-relevant sensor behavior
large synthetic data volume without curation discipline
tsqbev_actions: use for future-state synthetic augmentation only after the perception metric path is trustworthy

entries.title: Cosmos-Transfer2.5
entries.core_trick: Translate between simulated and real-world visual domains while preserving control-relevant or physically meaningful structure.
entries.intuition: Transfer models are useful when synthetic data exists but its appearance or sensory statistics do not match the real data distribution well enough for training transfer.
ablation_takeaways: The win is narrowing the sim-to-real appearance gap, not adding richer reasoning.
ablation_nuggets: Use when simulation already exists and needs world-to-world transfer before training.
Keep geometry checks in place so transferred videos remain physically consistent.
apply_when: simulated data is available but domain shift blocks its usefulness
avoid_when: real data is already sufficient and the bottleneck is model architecture or supervision
entries.integration_pattern: Insert before synthetic data joins the main corpus; evaluate transferred outputs with strict consistency and relevance filters.
failure_modes: synthetic artifacts that confuse the student more than they help
tsqbev_actions: treat as a synthetic-data quality tool, not as a perception trunk component

entries.title: NVIDIA TensorRT Model Optimizer
entries.core_trick: Provide a unified optimization library for quantization, pruning, distillation, speculative decoding, and deployment-oriented model compression workflows.
entries.intuition: This is the systems layer that turns a proven model into a faster or cheaper one. It should follow a metric-credible model, not precede it.
ablation_takeaways: Optimization framework gains matter only after the base model and evaluation path are technically sound.
ablation_nuggets: Use for deployment hardening after the student frontier is real.
Keep it out of the critical path while architecture and supervision are still unstable.
apply_when: the frontier recipe is stable enough to justify quantization or pruning work
avoid_when: you are still trying to discover the right student or teacher architecture
entries.integration_pattern: Apply after the knowledge-first frontier model is selected; pair with official calibration and export sanity gates.
failure_modes: premature optimization on a weak or broken model
optimizing latency while silently regressing official perception metrics
tsqbev_actions: use only after the frontier student beats the current best control on official metrics

entries.title: TensorRT-LLM
entries.core_trick: Provide optimized inference kernels, runtimes, and serving patterns for large models, including sparse attention and long-context inference optimizations on NVIDIA hardware.
entries.intuition: TensorRT-LLM is not the perception student, but it is relevant to the research lab because the planner, critic, and giant teachers may need frontier serving efficiency.
ablation_takeaways: Serving efficiency matters for large control-plane and teacher models, but it does not substitute for better supervision or perception design.
ablation_nuggets: Use for planner/critic or giant teacher serving once the control plane needs higher throughput.
Keep it separate from the student perception runtime unless a clear migration path exists.
apply_when: control-plane or large-teacher inference throughput is now limiting the research loop
avoid_when: the active bottleneck is still the student architecture or data path
entries.integration_pattern: Treat as control-plane and teacher-serving substrate, not as the student AV perception runtime.
failure_modes: sinking engineering effort into serving while the model itself is still weak
coupling the control plane too tightly to a specific hardware stack too early
tsqbev_actions: use for planner/critic or giant teacher serving before trying to transplant any of its kernel assumptions into the perception student

entries.title: MambaVision
entries.core_trick: Combine Mamba-style sequence modeling with transformer components in a hybrid vision backbone to keep long-range capacity while preserving strong vision performance.
entries.intuition: This is a more realistic vision SSM option than blindly swapping every attention block for Mamba. Hybridization is the lesson: use SSMs where they help sequence efficiency while preserving transformer strengths where spatial detail matters.
ablation_takeaways: Hybrid SSM-transformer designs are often safer than pure replacements when dense spatial structure still matters.
ablation_nuggets: Treat as a later camera-student branch if DINOv3 and perspective supervision still overrun the deployment budget.
Do not replace the teacher with this just because it is newer.
apply_when: you need a more efficient high-capacity vision backbone candidate after the frontier teacher-supervision path is proven
avoid_when: the current bottleneck is still weak cross-modal supervision rather than backbone efficiency
entries.integration_pattern: Test as a later efficient camera student or teacher-lite branch, not before the DINOv3-based foundation path is understood.
failure_modes: restarting architecture churn before the teacher-distillation path is stabilized
misplacing SSM blocks where explicit spatial alignment is still needed
tsqbev_actions: consider only after the DINOv3 teacher path and temporal-memory separation are validated
